pullRequest.title: Use `tl.exp2` for all gating operations
pullRequest.body:
In https://github.com/triton-lang/triton/issues/2893

> exp2 is faster than exp because the special function unit inside the GPU implements exp2. When you use the exp intrinsic, it expands to a multiplication by 1/log(2) followed by the exp2 instruction. In this case, we can fold this 1/log(2) multiplier into the sm_scale multiplier so that we need only perform one multiplication per element rather than two.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

- **New Features**
  - Added support for base-2 exponential functions with safe computation handling.
  - Introduced a new mathematical constant to improve accuracy in logarithmic and exponential operations.
  - Updated attention kernel calculations to utilize base-2 logarithms and exponentials, enhancing performance and precision.
  - Added optional scaling to cumulative sum operations, allowing enhanced flexibility in computations.
- **Tests**
  - Expanded test coverage for attention scaling factors to ensure robustness across different scale values.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pullRequest.title: [Models]: Add Comba Implementation
pullRequest.body:
Inspired by the implementation of Gated-DeltaNet, we restructured the Comba operator accordingly. A critical detail is that during the safe_exp computation in Comba, adding a small constant (1e-5) is essential to prevent large deviations during testing. To encapsulate this fix, we introduced the safe_exp_comba function in the utils.op

pullRequest.title: Fix kda.gate not enforcing contiguous mem layout
pullRequest.body:
Fix: https://github.com/fla-org/flash-linear-attention/issues/625

The `fla.kda.gate` triton kernel assumes the modeling framework will load the weights in contiguous memory. 

SGLang/vLLM may do this (I believe they almost always do this for performance reasons) but HF or other code that hook into the transformers modeling code which may move `weight` memory around due to offloading requirements may not. 

The pr simply ensures the conv1d weights/tensors are contiguous before launching triton kernel. There should be no performance impact as it is no-op if already contiguous. 

@yzhangcs

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved robustness of causal 1D convolution by adding input validation to the forward path, reducing runtime errors and improving stability during convolution operations.
  * Enhances safety for edge-case inputs and lowers likelihood of invalid-input failures in streaming or sequence-processing workflows.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pullRequest.title: Fix: Correct K/V dimension mismatch in path_attn bwd kernels"changing K/BK to V/BV for v and dv operations
pullRequest.body:
This PR fixes a critical dimension mismatch bug found in the `path_attn` backward pass kernels, which occurs when `BK != BV`.

**Related Issue:**
Fixes #632

**Description:**
The backward kernels (`parallel_path_bwd_inter_dkv` and `parallel_path_bwd_inter_dqh`) incorrectly assumed that the key dimension (`K`) and value dimension (`V`)—and their corresponding block sizes (`BK`, `BV`)—were always equal. The code was incorrectly using `K`/`BK` constants for operations on `V`/`BV`-dimensioned tensors.

This bug was likely masked during validation where `BK == BV` was the default. My testing, which intentionally set `BK=64` and `BV=128`, exposed this bug, leading to a `triton.compiler.errors.CompilationError` (as detailed in the linked issue).

**Changes:**

1.  **`fla/ops/path_attn/parallel_path_bwd_inter_dkv.py`**:
    * Corrected `tl.make_block_ptr` for `v` to use `V` and `BV`.
    * Corrected `tl.zeros` initialization for `b_dv` to use `BV`.
    * Corrected `tl.atomic_add` for `dv` to use the correct `V` and `BV` for strides and dimensions.

2.  **`fla/ops/path_attn/parallel_path_bwd_inter_dqh.py`**:
    * Corrected the `tl.make_block_ptr` for `v` to load with the proper `(BS, BV)` block size.
    * Applied `tl.trans(b_v)` within the `tl.dot` operation to ensure correct matrix multiplication dimensions (`[BS, BV] @ [BV, BS]`) for calculating `b_dp`.

pullRequest.title: Temporary workaround to disable TritonGPUHoistTMEMAlloc in b_dk += tl.dot(tl.trans(b_dA), b_kb)
pullRequest.body:
Temporarily fix https://github.com/fla-org/flash-linear-attention/issues/638

In the original code `b_dk += tl.dot(tl.trans(b_dA), b_kb)`, the compiler fuses the addition into the dot op, generating IR like %136 (b_dk new) = tt.dot %134 (tl.trans(b_dA)), %127 (b_kb), %135 (b_dk_old), using %135 as the accumulator.
It seems the `TritonGPUHoistTMEMAlloc` pass identifies that %135 (input) and %136 (output) share the same TMEM buffer and attempts an optimization. However, on B200, it incorrectly calculates the dominance, moving the definition of %135 after its use in %136, causing the `operand #2 does not dominate this use` error.
The inline_asm forces the compiler to separate the dot and add instructions. And seems like only inline_asm will work and all other methods like + 0.0, * 1.0 and even bitcasts will still lead to the compiler optimize them away and re-enable the fusion.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Fixed incorrect dot-product fusion on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs by adding a targeted runtime workaround to ensure correct math results.

* **New Features**
  * Added Blackwell device detection and a safe dot-product path that selects the appropriate implementation at runtime.

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pullRequest.title: [KDA] Add lowerbound gate function
pullRequest.body:
Reason:

- When using TensorCore with tile size M=16 for cumulative sum (cumsum) operations, the FP32 exp() function risks overflow when inputs exceed [-85, 85]. A cumsum across 16 tiles can easily violate this range.
- This PR adds a lower_bound parameter to constrain each tile's minimum value, ensuring the accumulated result stays within safe bounds. By limiting each tile to ≥ -5.0(for example), the worst-case accumulation (-5.0 × 16 = -80) remains safely within the [-85, 85] non-overflow zone for exp().
- Default behavior unchanged (lower_bound=None): Uses original `naive_kda_gate` (not recommended)
- With safety bound (lower_bound=-5.0): Uses `naive_kda_lowerbound_gate` with validated range [-5.0, 0.0)
- Recommendation: Set lower_bound=-5.0 for all TensorCore-based to prevent numerical instability

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added configurable lower-bound gating option for KDA operations and exposed it in fused gate interfaces
  * Added optimized chunked cumsum path with optional lower-bound behavior for varied sequence shapes

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Adjusted backward-path gradient handling for chunked gate computations to correct bias gradient flow

* **Tests**
  * Added tests covering the lower-bound gate variant across configurations

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pullRequest.title: [Cache] Fix get_seq_length to return per-layer length
pullRequest.body:
…al counter

This commit fixes a bug where FLACache.get_seq_length() returned a global _seen_tokens counter instead of delegating to individual layer's cache length. This diverged from Hugging Face's Cache class design and caused incorrect cache indexing in multi-layer models.

Changes:
- Add _seen_tokens counter to FLALayer to track per-layer sequence length
- Update FLALayer.update() to increment _seen_tokens from attn_state shape
- Update FLACache.get_seq_length() to delegate to layer.get_seq_length()
- Update FLACache.get_mask_sizes() to use per-layer seq_length
- Add tests for per-layer cache sequence length tracking

* **Tests**

pullRequest.title: [Mamba] Remove unused arguments and update to align with mamba_ssm
pullRequest.body:
Closes #780 

## Summary
Update the FLA Mamba implementations to more closely match the `mamba_ssm` reference behavior, modifying `Mamba`, `Mamba2`, and `LogLinearMamba2`.

- Align `fla/layers/mamba.py` with the reference `mamba_ssm` Mamba initialization behavior
- Refactor `fla/layers/mamba2.py` to better match the reference `mamba_ssm` API, initialization, normalization, and residual handling
- Update `fla/layers/log_linear_mamba2.py` to remain compatible with the new `Mamba2` behavior and execution paths
- Remove the unused `time_step_rank` argument from `Mamba2` and `LogLinearMamba2`

## Main Changes

### Mamba1
- Rename `time_step_rank` to `dt_rank` for consistency 
- Change `tdt_rank` to support `"auto"` rank selection
- Add explicit `dt` initialization parameters and reference-style `dt_proj` initialization
- Change default `use_bias` to `False`, matching `mamba_ssm` defaults
- Mark `A_log` and `D` as no-weight-decay parameters

### Mamba2
- Make `num_heads` inferable when not explicitly provided
- Add configurable convolution initialization and update default conv bias behavior
- Add `A_init_range`, `D_has_hdim`, `norm_before_gate`, and `time_step_init_floor`
- Change default `use_bias` to `False`
- Add explicit `dt_bias` initialization and no-weight-decay handling
- Support both per-head and per-head-dim `D` layouts
- Update fused and non-fused execution paths to handle:
  - optional RMSNorm
  - direct gating when RMSNorm is disabled
  - correct `D` reshaping across residual paths

### LogLinearMamba2

- Update helper scan functions to support:
  - optional gated `z`
  - squeezed single-token inputs
  - 1D or 2D `D`
- Align grouped norm and gating behavior with the new `Mamba2` implementation
- Update fast and scan paths for `D_has_hdim`, conditional RMSNorm, and direct gate application
- Remove unused `time_step_rank`

Files changed:

- `fla/layers/mamba.py`
- `fla/layers/mamba2.py`
- `fla/layers/log_linear_mamba2.py`

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Fine-grained dt controls (rank/limits/min/max/init_floor), optional conv weight init, optional per-head D layout, and configurable norm-before-gate behavior.
  * Toggleable RMSNorm vs gated-activation path; gate-derived path used when normalization is disabled.
  * Accepts inputs with implicit singleton sequence dimension and flexible gate/z handling.

* **Refactor**
  * Renamed time-step → dt parameters and updated public constructor APIs; unified D handling and conditional kernel arguments.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Removed unconditional errors for z/norm branches and added shape/reshape validations.

* **Tests**
  * Added exhaustive layer comparison tests (inference/train/eval/gradients) and test setup adjustments.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pullRequest.title: [GDN] Fix missing mask on off-diagonal blocks in fused kkt+s…
pullRequest.body:
Fix NaN in fused kkt+solve_tril kernel when seq_len % chunk_size != 0                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  Fixes #792                                                                                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  Problem                                                                                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  The fused chunk_gated_delta_rule_fwd_kkt_solve_kernel (introduced in baf57d52) produces NaN when the sequence length is not a multiple of chunk_size (64).                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  Root cause: Within a sub-chunk (BC=16) that partially exceeds the sequence boundary, out-of-bounds positions load g=0 via boundary_check, while in-bounds positions carry the cumsum'd gate        
  (potentially large negative values). Computing exp(0 - g_inbounds) = exp(large_positive) overflows to inf in float32. The corresponding K@K^T entries are 0 (K also loads as 0 for OOB), but IEEE  
  754 defines 0 * inf = NaN, which then corrupts the forward substitution.                                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  The old separate chunk_scaled_dot_kkt_fwd kernel was immune because it applied a single tl.where(m_A, b_A, 0) mask over the full 64×64 block after gate scaling, zeroing out all OOB entries. The  
  fused kernel operates on 16×16 sub-blocks and lacked this boundary masking.                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  Fix                                                                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  Add boundary masks (m_tc) into the tl.where guarding exp/exp2, so OOB positions are zeroed before the multiplication, preventing 0 * inf:                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  - Diagonal blocks: tl.where(m_d & m_tc_row & m_tc_col, exp(g_diff), 0.) — combines the strictly-lower-triangular mask with the boundary mask in a single tl.where                                  
  - Off-diagonal blocks: tl.where(m_tc_row & m_tc_col, exp(g_diff), 0.)                                                                                                                              
  - USE_G=False path: applies m_d to diagonal blocks (no exp, no boundary issue)                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  Verification                                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  - Existing 34 tests in test_gated_delta.py: all pass                                                                                                                                               
  - Targeted NaN test (T=75, large gate cumsum): 99 NaN without fix → 0 NaN with fix                                                                                                                 
  - Sweep over T∈{65,70,75,80,100,127,128,200,255,256} × gate_scale∈{50,100,200} × {exp, exp2}: 60/60 pass

pullRequest.title: [BugFix] Fix illegal memory access in KDA backward by dropping buggy autotune configs on Hopper
pullRequest.body:
This PR fixes #802.

## Problem
`chunk_kda_bwd_kernel_wy_dqkg_fused` triggers `CUDA error: an illegal memory access was encountered` during autotune on Hopper when `BK=32` and `num_warps=4`.

## Root Cause

Autotune configs with `BK=32` + `num_warps=4` are buggy on Hopper:
- BK=32, BV=64,  warps=4, stages=2/3/4 ❌
- BK=32, BV=128, warps=4, stages=2/3/4 ❌

All other configs (BK=32/warps=2, BK=64/warps=2/4) pass.

## Fix

Filter out the buggy configs on Hopper:

```python
if not (IS_NVIDIA_HOPPER and BK == 32 and num_warps == 4)
```

## Verification

The reproduction script from #802 now passes:
```python
from fla.layers.kda import KimiDeltaAttention
import torch
kda = KimiDeltaAttention().cuda().bfloat16()
x = torch.randn(size=(1, 128, 2048), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')
cu_seqlens = torch.tensor([0, 128], dtype=torch.int32, device='cuda')
out = kda(x, cu_seqlens=cu_seqlens)[0]
out.sum().backward()  # Backward passed!
```

pullRequest.title: [Fix] Fix incorrect cumsum dim for naive_chunk_linear_attn normalize
[Fix] GatedDeltaNet step mode crash when conv cache is None on cold Triton autotuner cache
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #859

## Problem

`ShortConvolution.step()` is triggered when `T=1` (single-token autoregressive generation). The null check for the conv state cache was gated on `output_final_state`:

```python
if output_final_state and cache is None:
    cache = x.new_zeros(N, D, W)
```

When `output_final_state=False` (the default), cache stays `None` and is passed directly to `causal_conv1d_update`. On a cold Triton autotuner disk cache, the autotuner benchmarks kernel configs and attempts to clone the input tensors to restore them after each trial. Since cache is `None`, this crashes with:

````
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'clone'
````

The bug is intermittent -- it only manifests on a cold autotuner cache (fresh environment, new container, or after `~/.triton/cache` is cleared). On warm cache runs the cached config is used directly and `None` is never cloned, masking the issue.

## Fix

Remove the `output_final_state` guard so the cache is always initialised when `None`:

```python
if cache is None:
    cache = x.new_zeros(N, D, W)
```

A zero-initialised cache is the correct starting state regardless of whether the caller wants the final state returned. This matches the initialisation behaviour of chunk mode and means callers are no longer required to pre-initialise state before the first step-mode call.
